revision_priority: Rewrite the Professional Summary into the required 3-sentence bold-header format and put the Amazon-relevant backend/selection bridge in sentence 1.
Split the Skills section into shorter, correctly named buckets and keep every line under the hard word limit.
Rename vague skills buckets into functional categories that a recruiter can parse quickly.
revision_instructions:
1) Leave all immutable job-history lines unchanged: TikTok must remain `Software Engineer Intern | TikTok · Security | Jun 2025 – Dec 2025 | San Jose, USA`; DiDi and Temu must also stay exact.
2) Replace the current `## Professional Summary` bullets with exactly 3 full sentences, each starting with a bold header in the form `**Header:** ...` and ending with a period.
3) Make sentence 1 the strongest role-aligned signal: backend/distributed-systems correctness, Java/Python/Go, and high-volume workflow validation; bring the closest Amazon-relevant bridge to the front.
4) Make sentence 2 cover automation and delivery depth: Playwright, Pytest, CI/CD, Docker, Kubernetes, GitHub Actions, and GitLab CI.
5) Keep sentence 3 as the Go 2-dan pattern-recognition line, but use a high-value cognitive header such as pattern recognition, decision-making, or systems judgment.
6) Rebuild `## Skills` into narrower, semantically correct buckets so each line stays under 14 words; if necessary, use 5 buckets to satisfy the hard limit.
7) Rename `APIs` to something accurate like `Testing`, `Delivery`, or `Tooling`; if `Cloud` still mixes infra and data stores, split it further or rename it to `Infra`.
8) Do not delete any skill that already has bullet evidence; instead redistribute the same technologies across shorter lines.
9) If you want Amazon Specialized Selection to feel credible, reframe one DiDi or Temu bullet toward marketplace/selection/routing/operational decisioning, but only with language that is already supported by the existing work.
